name: scout
_budget_tiers_doc: v8.13.5: declarative tiers selected via EVOLVE_TASK_MODE. 'research' covers Scout invocations doing 5+ web searches (motivated by cycle 8210's $0.51 burn). 'deep' covers exploratory cycles where Scout needs to read large portions of the codebase before proposing tasks. EVOLVE_MAX_BUDGET_USD always wins; tiers are a structured fallback below that.
_max_turns_design_note_: v9.0.3: lowered from 30 to 15 after cycle-11 evidence (49 turns / $1.32 — exceeded both old advisory caps). Design target is 8-12 turns; 15 leaves headroom for the few legitimate exploratory cycles. The advisory max_turns + persona-side ## Turn budget section together bound scout's discovery surface without stripping its tools. WebSearch/WebFetch retained for the fan-out 'research' sub-scout (parallel_subtasks); main-path scout is instructed by the persona to defer web work to Phase 1 RESEARCH.
_parallel_subtasks_doc: Sprint 1.1 fan-out: when invoked via `subagent-run.sh dispatch-parallel scout`, three sub-scouts run concurrently. Each writes to .evolve/runs/cycle-N/workers/scout-<name>.md and aggregator.sh merges into the canonical scout-report.md. Default-off — see EVOLVE_FANOUT_ENABLED in CLAUDE.md.

parallel_subtasks.name: codebase
parallel_subtasks.prompt_template: You are scout-codebase for cycle {cycle}. Focus exclusively on codebase analysis: read source files, dependency graphs, recent commits, and identify candidate task targets. DO NOT do web research. Your output is one of three sub-scout reports that will be merged.

parallel_subtasks.name: research
parallel_subtasks.prompt_template: You are scout-research for cycle {cycle}. Focus exclusively on web research: prior art, library documentation, competitor implementations relevant to the cycle's goal. DO NOT analyze the codebase. Your output is one of three sub-scout reports that will be merged.

parallel_subtasks.name: evals
parallel_subtasks.prompt_template: You are scout-evals for cycle {cycle}. Focus exclusively on eval definition design: propose 1-3 testable evals for the candidate tasks, with mutation-test specs ready for scripts/mutate-eval.sh. DO NOT do codebase grepping or web research beyond what's needed for eval design. Your output is one of three sub-scout reports that will be merged.

_parallel_eligible_reason: READ-ONLY summarizer (codebase scan, research, eval mining)
